posts.title: Hello World
posts.text: Welcome to Hexo! This is your very first post. Check documentation for more info. If you get any problems when using Hexo, you can find the answer in troubleshooting or you can ask me on GitHub. Quick StartCreate a new post1$ hexo new "My New Post" More info: Writing Run server1$ hexo server More info: Server Generate static files1$ hexo generate More info: Generating Deploy to remote sites1$ hexo deploy More info: Deployment

posts.title: first blog
posts.text: Okay, this is my first blogI’m not really sure whether I can renew this blog til I graduate haha.

posts.title: Idea and thought for Cubic Newton
posts.text: Cubic NewtonAfter having a conversation with Bo.J, I’ve change my direction to Cubic Newton which is a second-order algorithm. I’ve read Cubic Regularization of Newton written by Nestrov, and there are kinds of preponderence of CR: CR has a faster global convergence rate than Gradient Descent and local quadratic convergence. CR can jump out of Saddle point. CR have a great result in some weak convex problem such as Star Convex CR can keep necessary second order optimality condition. Difficulty of this algorithm is its subproblem, as this subproblem is a 1 dimenstional eqution with some tricky nonlinear function. The Tradtional method is Trust region method (but acutally I don’t really know this method) Adaptive Cubic NewtonThen A new method is Adaptive Cubic Regularization (ACR), this method is much relative to Nesterov’s method and there are some new tricky. This paper suggests that we should adaptively control the estimation of Lipschitz constant corresponding to the result of current update step. Besides, this paper use Krylov Subspace and a use estimatier of Hessian matrix to implement this job. How to learn it?First problem is how to guarantee the result satisfy second order principle. Some other difficulies concludes how to train? how to design objective function? etc. These days, we conceive an idea to solve subproblem:recall sub problem is to solve:$$ $$ Use Inverse Power Mthod to get minimum eigenvalue $\lambda_n$. Objective function( assume Finite K Iteration, and we have a penalty parameter $\gamma$ ):$$\sum_{i=1}^K(f(\hat{x}_i)+\gamma ^i(\sigma_i|\hat{x}i-\hat{x}{i-1}|-\lambda^{(i)}n))$$We use LSTMnn to generate a sequential vector $s_k$, where$x{k+1}=x_k+s_k$ How to testin test part, we’ll consider the adaptive strategy suggested in this paper.More specific, if the result of s_k is worse than GD, then we use GD to update this step.

posts.title: Idea and thought for Cubic Newton
posts.text: GD to GDThis is a ground breaking job for L2O, as it firstly suggest use DNN to generate a update rule for optimization. The idea is quite simple: All optimizer cannot be always more efficient than stochastic optimizer, which means need of adjust opimizer according specific case, and RNN can do this job. Authors use LSTM to generate a update step according gradient information.There are many varients inspired by this job and the latest one is to represent a update step as a linear function such as DX+B where X is gradient, then we can use LSTM to generate D and B. This method is more stablized. Inspiration for meI can learn a matrx A according gradient, and use A’A as a substitution of Hessen Matrix to use Newton update step. Or I can use gradint and truth Hessen Marix to generate new step? Maybe I still need consider a more elaberated framework to learning, but I think it will be a practice for me and I’ll try to realize it by PyTorch.

pages.text: Thank you for browsing my blog!I’m Sevan Li, a student learning at SUFE. This blog will record what I wanna say, what I feel impressively or ideas&knowleadge I gain through research.You can contact me by email, and my email is:li.hongpei@stu.sufe.edu.cn
